last_result.result: EasyCrypt accepted this read-only probe. The committed proof state was not changed; `goal_after_probe` shows the goal that would be visible if this tactic were committed.
last_result.effect: This is a read-only probe; it asks EasyCrypt what would happen without changing the committed proof state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

probe_preview.effect: This preview is speculative only; the committed EasyCrypt proof state was not changed.

last_result.message: This structural transition probe was accepted.

structural_transition.decision: Commit this exact accepted tactic only if you want to enter the post-tactic phase.
structural_transition.after_commit: The next authoritative view will show the real workbench after this tactic is committed.
structural_transition.if_wrong_after_commit: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

last_result.question: Do you want to enter the post-tactic workbench?
last_result.guidance: If yes, commit this exact tactic and read the real post-commit workbench. If no, choose another probe or inspect the current state. Do not solve the speculative preview in your mental model.
last_result.if_route_wrong: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

moves.title: Concrete tactic candidate
moves.applicability: This tactic is already concrete for the current goal.
moves.effect: Committing this tactic would change the EasyCrypt proof state if EasyCrypt accepts it.
evidence: Daemon probe accepted this tactic and it would leave 1 subgoal(s); commit it if that is the intended decomposition.
EasyCrypt accepted this tactic in a read-only probe on the current goal; committing it would still be a separate proof-state change.

moves.guarantee: not verified on the current goal; probe before committing; reversible

probe_alternatives.how_to_use: This tactic was accepted by a read-only probe on the current proof state. The transition record describes the checked effect and any visible limitations.

structural_transition.decision: Commit this exact accepted tactic only if you want to enter the post-tactic phase.
structural_transition.after_commit: The next authoritative view will show the real workbench after this tactic is committed.
structural_transition.if_wrong_after_commit: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

checks: Pr resources are context: use them when they match the visible endpoint and simplify the goal.
Direct byequiv is a valid route when the goal is naturally a program equivalence.

obligation_families.effect: The current proof point is dominated by local logical obligations rather than program-frontier movement.
obligation_families.limitations: Quantifier names are not normalized across EasyCrypt pretty-printer variants.

translation_facts: Decorations identify the program memory from which a term is read.
Pretty-printer memory names and introduced proof-context names can differ.
ambient_memory_translation.effect: Local claims in the pure tail depend on the currently introduced memory binders as well as displayed term decorations.

pure_tail_surface.effect: Summarizes verifier-visible pure-goal structure after program frontier actions have been discharged or hidden by local proof steps.
limitations: This panel records syntactic and proof-state facts from the current view.
It does not choose a tactic or instantiate a lemma.

selected_handles.title: Concrete tactic candidate
selected_handles.why_relevant: Daemon probe accepted this tactic and it would leave 1 subgoal(s); commit it if that is the intended decomposition.
selected_handles.derivation: surfaced from current-state analysis; not daemon-checked on this goal
selected_handles.guarantee: not verified on the current goal; probe before committing; reversible

selected_handles.title: Read-only tactic probe
selected_handles.why_relevant: goal parser reports a leading implication/quantifier
selected_handles.derivation: read-only probe surfaced from current-state analysis
selected_handles.guarantee: read-only probe shape; proof state unchanged; probe then decide; reversible

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

evidence: pure_tail_surface is visible for the current goal
current proof state has no program-frontier work before the logical residual
limitations: does not select a destructor, witness, rewrite, or SMT lemma
reported checkpoints are recovery references, not a default route

items.source: discarded suffix saved before checkpoint rewind

available_chunks.effect: Scratch replay checks this old route chunk from the current committed proof state.
available_chunks.limitations: A changed invariant or branch shape can make only a prefix of the old chunk replayable.

items.interpretation: These chunks are old committed tactics after a rewind. A probe checks a chunk in a scratch verifier session before any state-changing replay commit.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Current Pr equality may need a manager-verified bridge route (game-hop or scheme/endpoint normalization) before direct byequiv/proc lowering.
ask_manager_for.returns: daemon-verified, committable Pr bridge routes with manager-owned intent payloads

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need pRHL/procedure-equivalence bridge lemma names or context after checking pr_bridge_routes.
ask_manager_for.returns: context-only equiv bridge lemma candidates (names to look up/apply); not a verified Pr route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need local lemma hints before choosing a Pr-level proof route.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and proof-route context

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need exact byphoare/phoare-loop tactic syntax before probing the probability route.

items.label: Restore before last checkpoint rewind
items.why_checkpoint: state saved before the last checkpoint rewind; selecting it returns to that proof state
items.effect_if_selected: This restores the branch to the proof state saved before the last checkpoint rewind.
